```{r}
                                 labels = c(expression(N[0]), expression(N[2]), expression(N[6])))

dat_ll1$mesh_size <- factor(dat_ll1$mesh_size,levels = c("0.07 mm", "2 mm", "5 mm"))


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
dat_ml1<-dat_ll1 %>% group_by(Nitrogen_added, mesh_size)%>%
  dplyr::summarise(ml= mean(loss_Tannin),
                   sd= sd(loss_Tannin),
                   se=sd(loss_Tannin,na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(n()))

Cond_decay=ggplot(dat_ml1,aes(mesh_size,ml,color=Nitrogen_added))+
  geom_rect(data=NULL,aes(xmin=1.5,xmax=2.5,ymin=-Inf,ymax=Inf),
            fill="#E8E8E8", colour = NA, alpha = 0.1)+
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(0.9),stat="identity",size=3)+
  geom_errorbar(ymax=dat_ml1$ml+dat_ml1$se,ymin=dat_ml1$ml-dat_ml1$se, width=0.3, position = position_dodge(0.9))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,6))+
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0, label = "a")+
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 0, label = "a")+
  annotate("text", x = 1.25, y = 0, label = "b")+
  #annotate("text", x = 1.75, y = 0, label = "ab")+
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 0, label = "ns")+
  #annotate("text", x = 2.25, y = 0, label = "b")+
  #annotate("text", x = 2.75, y = 0, label = "a")+
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 0, label = "ns")+
  #annotate("text", x = 3.25, y =0, label = "b")+
  scale_color_manual(values =c("#576904","#A98645","#ADFF2F"),
                     labels = c(expression(N[0]), expression(N[2]), expression(N[6])))+
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  labs( colour="N addition")+
  xlab("Mesh size")+
  ylab(expression(paste( "Tannins decomposition constant"~~ ( yrs^ -1 ))))+guides(color=guide_legend(order=2),shape=guide_legend(order=1))+
  theme(plot.title =element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=(11)))+
  theme(
    legend.direction = "vertical",
    legend.text = element_text(size=11))+
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=(11)))+
  theme(axis.title = element_text(family = "palatino", size = (11), colour = "black"))+
  theme(axis.text = element_text(family = "palatino", colour = "black", size = (12)))+ 
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", size = 0.5),
        axis.ticks.x=element_line(colour = "black"),
        axis.ticks.y=element_line(colour = "black"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA,color="black", size=0.5, linetype="solid"),
        panel.background = element_blank())

#Cond_decay


ggsave(filename="Figure Conddensed tannins decay constant.png", plot=Cond_decay, device="tiff",
       height=3.5, width=4, units="in", dpi=600)
```


#### one-way analysis of variance'mesh_size'

```{r}
dat_ll1$transrate=log(dat_ll1$loss_Tannin)
dat_ll1_fine=dat_ll1[dat_ll1$mesh_size=="0.07 mm",]
dat_ll1_med=dat_ll1[dat_ll1$mesh_size=="2 mm",]
dat_ll1_big=dat_ll1[dat_ll1$mesh_size=="5 mm",]
result <- aov(dat_ll1_fine$transrate ~ dat_ll1_fine$Nitrogen_added, data = dat_ll1_fine)
summary(result)
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(result)
print(tukey_result)
result <- aov(dat_ll1_med$transrate ~ dat_ll1_med$Nitrogen_added, data = dat_ll1_med)
summary(result)
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(result)
print(tukey_result)
result <- aov(dat_ll1_big$transrate ~ dat_ll1_big$Nitrogen_added, data = dat_ll1_big)
summary(result)
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(result)
print(tukey_result)

```



#### Establish the lmm model

```{r}
##Inverse normal transformationï¼Inverse Normal Transformationï¼
hist(dat_ll1$loss_Tannin)


##Run package
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(pbkrtest)
##Build full model Consider interaction
mod4=lmer(data=dat_ll1, transrate~Nitrogen_added*mesh_size+(1|species))
summary(mod4)
anova(mod4)

###ä¼¼ç¶æ¯æ£éªæ¨¡å
model_no_3way <- update(mod4, . ~ . - mesh_size:Nitrogen_added)
anova(model_no_3way)
anova(mod4,model_no_3way)

###r2æ£éª
library(MuMIn)
r2 <- r.squaredGLMM(mod4)
r2
##Diagnostic model
plot(mod4)
qqnorm(resid(mod4), main = "Q-Qå¾ï¼æ®å·®ï¼")



```

```{r}
cor_test <- cor.test(dat_ll1$k, dat_ll1$loss_Tannin)
cor_value <- round(cor_test$estimate, 3)
cor_value
p_value <- round(cor_test$p.value, 4)
cor_test$p.value
```

```{r}
Mass_decay_vs_Cond_decay=ggplot()+ geom_point(data = dat_ll1, 
                        aes(y = loss_Tannin , x =k),
                        alpha = 0.5, 
                        size = 3) + 
  geom_smooth(data=dat_ll1,method = "lm",colour= "black" ,se=TRUE, aes(x=k , y= loss_Tannin),size=1,linetype=3)+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,6))+
  #scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,4))+
  labs(colour ="Mesh size")+ 
  xlab( expression(paste("Decomposition constant k " ~( yrs^ -1 ))))+ylab( expression(paste("Tannins decomposition constant "~( yrs^ -1 ) )))+
  #annotate("text", x = 2, y = 3.5, size=4,label = "italic(R^2)==0.384~~~italic(p)<0.01", parse=TRUE, colour= "black" ) +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.2,0.85))+
  theme(plot.title =element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=(12)))+
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", size = 0.5),
        axis.ticks.x=element_line(colour = "black"),
        axis.ticks.y=element_line(colour = "black"),
        axis.ticks.length = unit(-0.15, "cm"),
        axis.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 10),
        axis.title = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA,color="black", size=0.5, linetype="solid"),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.text=element_text(size=10,face="bold.italic"),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key.width=unit(0.2,"cm"),
        legend.key.height=unit(0.2,"cm"))+
  guides(fill="none")+
  scale_color_manual(values =c("#576904","#A98645","#ADFF2F","#A45378","#EFC000"))
#Mass_decay_vs_Cond_decay


ggsave(filename="Figure 4 Mass decay constant and Condensed tannins decay constant.png", plot=Mass_decay_vs_Cond_decay, device="tiff",
       height=4, width=4, units="in", dpi=600)


```


Create fig2.fig3,fig4,table3

```{r}
Fig2<-(Carb_decay+Nitr_decay)/(Phos_decay+Pota_decay)
ggsave(filename="Figure 2.png", plot=Fig2, device="tiff",
       height=3, width=9, units="in", dpi=600)
Fig2

```

Figure 2

```{r}
Fig3<-(Calc_decay+Magn_decay)/(Mang_decay+Cond_decay)
ggsave(filename="Figure 3.png", plot=Fig3, device="tiff",
       height=3, width=9, units="in", dpi=600)
Fig3

```

Figure 3


```{r}
Fig4<-(Mass_decay_vs_Carb_decay+Mass_decay_vs_Nitr_decay)/(Mass_decay_vs_Phos_decay+Mass_decay_vs_Pota_decay)/Mass_decay_vs_Magn_decay
ggsave(filename="Figure 4.png", plot=Fig2, device="tiff",
       height=3, width=9, units="in", dpi=600)
Fig4

```

Figure 4

# Conclusions

Nitrogen addition level, litter quality, mesh size and their interactions significantly influenced the litter decomposition rates. As for litter decomposition rate, macrofauna showed higher sensitivity to nitrogen addition, while mesofauna and microorganisms exhibited lower sensitivity, particularly under high nitrogen addition treatments. Nitrogen addition significantly influenced the release rates of carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, magnesium, and condensed tannin, litterbags mesh size significantly affected the release rates of carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, and magnesium. Nitrogen addition promotes the release of nitrogen from litter, while high levels of nitrogen addition inhibit the release of elements such as, calcium and magnesium, particularly pronounced in the presence of macrofauna. This indicates that when predicting the impacts of global environmental changes such as N deposition on ecosystem processes (e.g., nutrient cycling), the functional trait distribution (e.g., body size) within the biological community must be explicitly considered. Scientific evidence can be provided to support sustainable forest management.
